key_insights.insight: We often prioritize connection as an easier fix for internal wounds, leading to potentially broken hearts and abuse.
key_insights.quote: We feel that if I'm with someone, I'm going to feel better about myself than if I have to do that work myself… it leads to broken hearts, it leads to abuse, it leads to manipulation and control.

key_insights.insight: Strive for a definition of success based on internal joy that generates joy for others.
key_insights.quote: It comes back to a part of the definition of success, which for me is doing something that gives me joy and makes me happy that as a byproduct gives other people joy and makes them happy.

key_insights.insight: If you're struggling in a relationship, examine your own values and the values of your partner.
key_insights.quote: I honestly feel that over the last three years, I haven't been able to understand, I haven't really been able to gain context of why you've made the choices you've made. And I really want to understand anddeeply and there for you to understand if these choices are aligned with your values. If they are,those are not the values that attract me.

key_insights.insight: Stop trying to change your partner, instead aim to understand their vision and see if it matches your own.
key_insights.quote: Stop trying to change anyone because people don't change for people, they change for themselves.  Like stop trying to change anyone.

key_insights.insight: If you are only using your beauty to attract someone, that's the value you're going to attract.
key_insights.quote: If you only use your physical appearance and you believe that's your only asset and you're playing the game of just showing off that, guess what you're going to attract?

key_insights.insight: True self-worth comes from doing hard things and recognizing the growth made during those times.
key_insights.quote: Self-value real self-worth comes from doing hard things… self-worth and self-value comes from recognizingthe hardship you've been throughand the growth that you made during that time.

key_insights.insight: Sex is a byproduct of intimacy and a healthy connection; do not use it as a crutch.
key_insights.quote: Great sex is a byproduct of great connection and intimacy. It's not a replacement for or a source of.

key_insights.insight: Porn can rewire your brain with false expectations and diminishing returns for pleasure.
key_insights.quote: All the studies that I did look at show that porn is making you work harder. You're going to have to find something more extreme… You're now rewiring your brain to not feel as much pleasure from normal sex.

actionable_advice.advice: Discover new things about your partner by putting yourselves in a vulnerable state together.
actionable_advice.how_to: Do an escape room, try a new sport, go to an art class or pottery together

actionable_advice.advice: Spend time with your partner where you share energy and interact together, not just passively being in each other's presence.
actionable_advice.how_to: Set a monthly rule where you spend three days together, device-free, somewhere away from home.

actionable_advice.advice: Discover what you like and dislike about yourself as well as what is valuable to you.
actionable_advice.how_to: Dedicate 3 months to thinking about your likes and dislikes, values and goals.

actionable_advice.advice: If you want to stop or significantly curb your usage of porn, be honest with yourself why you're doing it.
actionable_advice.how_to: Try to use the energy you normally use chasing someone to create something you enjoy.

actionable_advice.advice: Delay having sex with someone you're dating.
actionable_advice.how_to: Commit for a number of months to date without sex in order to determine if there is real intimacy, connection and something to build upon.

memorable_stories: Jay describes taking seven days to process UK media criticism, and how he analyzed his own feelings.
Jay recounts the story of a student giving either 100 pounds to one person or 1 pound to one hundred people, illustrating the varying values of giving and receiving.
Jay shares the fable of a boy discovering the worth of a stone as he goes to a baker, an antique shop, and a jeweller.
contrarian_claims: If you are only dating someone because you're in love with who someone could be, you're not in love with them.
Prioritize Dharma (purpose) before Karma (relationships), and that self-sacrifice to a partner ultimately leads to resentment.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Jay Shetty: 8 Rules For Perfect Love & Amazing Sex!
